<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Analyze all board-changing transitions to understand mechanics

for i, t in enumerate(transitions):
    if t.after_frame.segmentation['nodes'] != t.before_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
        before_nodes = {n['color']: n['pixels'] for n in t.before_frame.segmentation['nodes'] if n['boundary'][0][0] < 63}
        after_nodes = {n['color']: n['pixels'] for n in t.after_frame.segmentation['nodes'] if n['boundary'][0][0] < 63}
        print(f"  [{i}] {t.action}:")
        print(f"    Before: {before_nodes}")
        print(f"    After:  {after_nodes}")
</parameter>
</function>

</function>

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: The initial state had N=14px and Y=23px seeds. My 80 actions have shrunk them to N=5px and Y=5px. The target is likely N=7px and Y=7px to match the container shapes. I need to find actions that GROW the seeds rather than shrink them.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python

<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try clicking on the left N shape in the N container to see if it grows the N seed
# The left N shape is at rows 20-22, cols 38-40
# Let's click on (20, 39) which is the top-middle of the left N shape
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 20, 'col': 39}])

# Check the result
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N' and n['boundary'][0][0] < 30:
        print(f"N seed: {n['pixels']}px, center~{n['boundary']}")
    if n['color'] == 'Y' and n['boundary'][0][0] < 35:
        print(f"Y seed: {n['pixels']}px, center~{n['boundary']}")
